title: tests/perf/ suite enforcing performance budgets
description: Gated perf suite that benchmarks /api/tracks, generate-apply, health, classify on a synthetic 10k-track sandbox DB. Fails if any §2 budget regresses by >20%. Run via pytest -m perf or RUN_PERF=1.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/perf/ directory contains: test_tracks_endpoint.py, test_generate_apply.py, test_health_endpoint.py, test_classify.py, test_similar_index.py, test_auto_tag.py, test_enrich.py.
All tests use pytest.mark.perf and skip by default (gated by RUN_PERF=1).
10k-track sandbox DB is built via tests/perf/conftest.py fixture (in-memory or temp file).
Each test asserts against the §2 budget table with a 20% headroom (so test fails on >20% regression but tolerates noise).
Tests output a summary table on success (timing, throughput).
GitHub Actions workflow runs perf suite nightly (not on every PR) to detect regressions over time.

steps.details: tests/perf/conftest.py: fixture build_10k_db() copies the test sandbox + duplicates rows to reach 10k DjmdContent + proportional DjmdSongHistory + DjmdSongMyTag. Cached to tests/perf/_cache_10k.db across runs to avoid rebuilding.
tests/perf/test_tracks_endpoint.py: time first call (cold snapshot rebuild) - assert <= 800ms * 1.2; time second call (warm) - assert <= 200ms * 1.2.
tests/perf/test_generate_apply.py: time full 3k-track apply; assert sustained >= 50 tracks/sec * 0.8 (= 40 tracks/sec floor). Also assert no DB lock contention errors.
Cold: empty CacheStore; assert >= 30 * 0.8 = 24/sec. Warm: pre-populate CacheStore; assert >= 500 * 0.8 = 400/sec.
Cold cache: <= 30s * 1.2 = 36s (initial budget pre-cache). Warm cache: <= 5s * 1.2 = 6s.
Pytest plugin or simple conftest hook to collect timings and print a summary table at end-of-session.
.github/workflows/perf.yml: runs nightly on schedule (cron); uses RUN_PERF=1; uploads timing summary as artifact; fails the workflow on budget violation.

technicalNotes: Perf tests on shared CI runners are noisy; the 20% headroom + nightly cadence (not PR-blocking) tame this.
Cached 10k sandbox DB is critical - rebuilding it on every run would itself take ~30s.
If a PR introduces a true regression > 20%, the nightly job catches it within 24h and creates an issue (or maintainer reviews the run).
